# Logister

Logister is an open source, self-hosted error monitoring and bug triage app for teams that want a forkable alternative to Bugsnag, Sentry, and Bugzilla-style workflows.

The primary goal is helping teams run Logister themselves. The public hosted app at logister.org is a secondary option.

The docs are organized as a self-hosted product manual: Documentation, Installation, Configuration, Usage, Integrations, API reference, and Troubleshooting.

It supports:
- Ruby and Rails apps via the `logister-ruby` gem
- .NET and ASP.NET Core apps via the `logister-dotnet` SDK
- Python apps via the `logister-python` package
- JavaScript and TypeScript apps via the `logister-js` package
- Android apps via the `org.logister:logister-android` Maven Central package
- iOS apps via the `Logister` Swift Package Manager package
- CFML apps running on Lucee or Adobe ColdFusion via direct HTTP ingestion
- Cloudflare Pages sites via direct HTTP telemetry today and project importer settings ahead of automatic fetching

Canonical documentation lives at the public docs URL:
- Product docs: https://logister.org/docs/
- Docs AI map: https://logister.org/docs/llms.txt
- Docs full AI context: https://logister.org/docs/llms-full.txt
- Docs sitemap: https://logister.org/docs/sitemap.xml
- Self-host source: https://github.com/taimoorq/logister

- Terms of Use: /terms

## Self-hosting and release distribution
- Source to fork or self-host: https://github.com/taimoorq/logister
- License: MIT License at https://github.com/taimoorq/logister/blob/main/LICENSE
- Brand policy: Logister name, logo, wordmark, visual identity, and brand assets are not licensed for use in forks or redistributed versions. Public forks should replace Logister branding with their own branding. See https://github.com/taimoorq/logister/blob/main/TRADEMARKS.md
- GitHub Releases: https://github.com/taimoorq/logister/releases
- GHCR image package: https://github.com/taimoorq/logister/pkgs/container/logister
- Docker Hub image package: https://hub.docker.com/r/taimoorq/logister
- Optional Quay image mirror: `quay.io/taimoorq/logister` when Quay credentials are configured in the release workflow
- Versioned Docker images: `ghcr.io/taimoorq/logister:v2.8` or `docker.io/taimoorq/logister:v2.8`
- Latest Docker images: `ghcr.io/taimoorq/logister:latest` or `docker.io/taimoorq/logister:latest`
- Docker self-hosting docs: https://logister.org/docs/self-hosting/#docker
- Deployment and `.env.sample` reference: https://logister.org/docs/deployment/
- GitHub App source integration: https://logister.org/docs/github-app/
- Notification paths: https://logister.org/docs/product/notifications/
- Data retention: https://logister.org/docs/product/data-retention/
- Release and discovery runbooks: https://github.com/taimoorq/logister/tree/main/docs

## Product summary
- Captures and groups application errors into triageable error groups.
- Stores stacktrace, request method, request URL/path, request context, occurrence history, assignment, status, release, environment, and source/deployment context when integrations provide it.
- Supports optional GitHub App source integration for private repository source excerpts, CODEOWNERS hints, repository sync, deployment links, and GitHub issue creation when Issues write permission is granted.
- Accepts `error`, `log`, `metric`, `transaction`, `span`, and `check_in` events, plus deployment records through `/api/v1/deployments`.
- Public ingestion APIs accept 1,200 requests per minute per API token per endpoint by default for `/api/v1/ingest_events`, `/api/v1/check_ins`, and `/api/v1/deployments`; missing or invalid authentication is capped at 120 failures per minute per source IP. Limit responses use `429 Too Many Requests` with retry headers. App admins listed in `LOGISTER_ADMIN_EMAILS` can set project-level overrides; project owners and shared project members cannot.
- Provides a cross-app dashboard with server-backed explorer charts, needs-attention summaries, active-project shortcuts, and assigned-to-me links.
- Helps teams triage grouped errors, assign owners, filter inboxes by assignee, inspect event context, connect related logs, review transaction and span-backed performance, watch scheduled work, receive purpose-specific project notifications, share project visibility, archive projects without losing history, and operate the self-hosted app.
- Archived projects remain accessible from archived/all project views, are hidden from active dashboards and top navigation, and have active API tokens revoked.
- Includes a public docs site with product, setup, deployment, API, infrastructure planning, and integration guides.
- Supports self-hosting with Rails, PostgreSQL, Redis, Sidekiq, SMTP/Amazon SES, optional S3-compatible archive storage, optional ClickHouse, GHCR and Docker Hub release images, and optional Quay mirrors.
- Storage and retention: PostgreSQL remains the primary system of record; optional ClickHouse stores raw events/spans and one-minute rollups for analytics; project retention settings can keep hot telemetry bounded; optional S3-compatible archive storage can store compressed JSONL exports before pruning older non-error hot telemetry.

## Primary audience
- Teams who want one self-hosted dashboard for seeing app issues, investigating them, and fixing them faster.
- Teams evaluating whether to run their own Logister instance before using the secondary hosted app.
- Teams comparing open source alternatives to Bugzilla, Bugsnag, Sentry, and other paid app monitoring or issue triage products.
- Python developers instrumenting APIs, workers, schedulers, and Python logging.
- .NET developers instrumenting ASP.NET Core apps and C# workers.
- JavaScript and TypeScript developers integrating Node or Express apps.
- Android developers integrating Kotlin or Java Android apps.
- iOS developers integrating Swift apps with Swift Package Manager.
- ColdFusion, Lucee, and CFML developers sending structured events to Logister.
- Rails teams looking for self-hosted exception grouping, request context, and assignment.

## Companion packages
- RubyGems: https://rubygems.org/gems/logister-ruby
- Ruby source: https://github.com/taimoorq/logister-ruby
- .NET source: https://github.com/taimoorq/logister-dotnet
- .NET base package: https://www.nuget.org/packages/Logister
- .NET ASP.NET Core package: https://www.nuget.org/packages/Logister.AspNetCore
- PyPI: https://pypi.org/project/logister-python/
- Python source: https://github.com/taimoorq/logister-python
- npm: https://www.npmjs.com/package/logister-js
- JavaScript source: https://github.com/taimoorq/logister-js
- Maven Central: https://central.sonatype.com/artifact/org.logister/logister-android
- Android source: https://github.com/taimoorq/logister-android
- Android coordinates: `org.logister:logister-android:0.1.2`
- Swift Package Manager source: https://github.com/taimoorq/logister-ios.git
- iOS release: https://github.com/taimoorq/logister-ios/releases/tag/v0.1.3
- Cloudflare Pages integration docs: https://logister.org/docs/integrations/cloudflare-pages/
- Maintained SDKs can send source context (`repository`, `commit_sha`, and `branch`) on events. JavaScript, Python, .NET, and Ruby SDKs also expose deployment-record helpers; Android and iOS attach source context in-app and expect CI/CD to POST deployment records to `/api/v1/deployments`.

## Self-host source
- App source: https://github.com/taimoorq/logister
- Full AI context: https://logister.org/llms-full.txt
